replacements.name: sisalto
replacements.defaultValue:
Congratulations!

You have been accepted to study in the above-mentioned programme in the spring 2026 joint application to upper secondary education and preparatory education.

Please remember to accept your study place no later than 25 June 2026. If you do not accept the place by the deadline, you will lose the study place reserved for you.

You will receive an email containing a link through which you can accept the study place. Alternatively, you may accept the study place by logging in to the My Studyinfo (Oma Opintopolku) service. Logging in requires strong authentication (e.g. online banking credentials). Please note that the educational institution may also require you to accept the study place in writing or by registering in person. Carefully read the instructions in the admission letter sent to you by the institution by post or email.

This letter also contains information about the status of your other application preferences in the student admission process. Application preferences ranked lower than the one to which you have been accepted have been automatically cancelled, and you can no longer be accepted to them. However, you may still be accepted from a waiting list to any application preferences ranked higher. Information about the admission criteria and scoring factors can be found in Studyinfo under Student admission in the joint application to upper secondary education and preparatory education.

replacements.name: liiteotsikko
replacements.defaultValue: Your situation in the spring 2026 joint application to upper secondary education and preparatory education

replacements.name: sis2
replacements.defaultValue:
How to Read the Table

Your Application Preferences

All your application preferences in the order you listed them on your application.

Accepted / All Applicants

The number of applicants accepted to the programme in relation to the total number of applicants.

Your Score / Lowest Accepted Score

The total score consists of several components. Information about the criteria and scoring factors can be found in Studyinfo under Student admission in the joint application to upper secondary education and preparatory education. The lowest accepted score is the minimum score with which applicants have been admitted to the programme. If you applied with a foreign certificate, without a school-leaving certificate, or if you have studied mathematics and mother tongue according to an individualised curriculum, no score will be calculated for you.

If you applied for vocational education provided with demanding special support, preparatory education and training for work and independent living (TELMA), preparatory education for vocational education (TUVA), a folk high school study year programme or general upper secondary education in English, no score will be calculated for you.

Entrance and Aptitude Test Result

If you participated in an entrance or aptitude test, your test score is shown here. If you applied with a foreign certificate, without a school-leaving certificate, or with an individualised curriculum in mathematics and mother tongue, you applied through discretionary admission, and no score will be calculated for you. Further information about the results can be obtained directly from the institution where you took the test.

Admission Result

The admission result may be accepted, accepted through discretionary admission, on a waiting list, you were not offered admission, or cancelled. The result is marked as cancelled if you have been accepted to a higher-ranked application preference.

If your status is on a waiting list, your waiting list position is shown in this column. If your result is you were not offered admission or cancelled, you cannot be on a waiting list.

Additional Information on Admission Results

Tie-Breaking in Vocational Education

In case of a tie, applicants to vocational education are ranked according to the following criteria:

  1. Order of application preferences
  2. Score from any entrance or aptitude test
  3. Overall academic performance
  4. Weighted grades

After this, applicants with identical scores are selected in a random order.

Tie-Breaking in General Upper Secondary Education (Upper Secondary School)

Upper secondary schools select students based on grade point average. If several applicants have the same average, the selection is decided by:

  1. Order of application preferences
  2. Grade point average calculated from academic subjects as well as physical education, crafts, home economics, music, and visual arts

After this, applicants with identical scores are selected in a random order.

Discretionary Admission

The education provider may admit an applicant regardless of score for a special reason. If you applied through discretionary admission due to social reasons, learning difficulties, or insufficient language skills, you also participate in the normal score-based selection process. If your prior education was completed abroad, you do not have a school-leaving certificate, or you studied mathematics and your mother tongue according to an individualised curriculum, you can only be admitted through discretionary admission. In this case, the admission result is either accepted through discretionary admission or you were not offered admission.

Contact Information

Further information is available in Studyinfo (studyinfo.fi). If you need help interpreting the result letter or if there are uncertainties related to the selection that cannot be resolved by contacting the institution directly, you may contact Studyinfo application advisory services at neuvonta@opintopolku.fi or by phone at +358 29 533 1010. Telephone advisory services are available on 11–12 June from 9:30 a.m. to 1:00 p.m.

Right of Appeal

If you are dissatisfied with the result of the student selection, you may request rectification of the selection decision from The Finnish Supervisory Agency. To do so, you must request a written decision from the educational institution whose selection decision you wish to appeal. The decision must be requested within 30 days of receiving notification of this result. The request for rectification must be submitted within 14 days of receiving the decision. An appeal for rectification concerning a student admission decision must be submitted to The Finnish Supervisory Agency. The request for rectification is made using an electronic form, but it can also be sent by email to kirjaamo@lvv.fi or by post to The Finnish Supervisory Agency, P.O. Box 20, FI-13035 LVV, Finland. More detailed instructions can be found on the website of The Finnish Supervisory Agency. If, for any reason, you do not receive the decision from the institution, please contact Studyinfo application advisory services.

replacements.name: subject
replacements.defaultValue: Notification of the results in the joint application to upper secondary education and preparatory education

replacements.name: email_content
replacements.defaultValue:
Dear Applicant,

You have applied in the spring 2026 joint application to upper secondary education and preparatory education. The admission results in the joint application are now ready. You can download and print your student admission result letter by clicking the following link:

replacements.name: email_content2
replacements.defaultValue:
The link is valid until 31 December 2026. Please note that the results shown in the downloadable result letter reflect the situation as of 11 June. Any changes in admissions (e.g. changes in waiting list positions) will not be updated in the result letter downloaded via this link.

If you are on a waiting list, your admission status may change daily. If you are accepted from the waiting list after this message has been sent, you will receive a separate notification.

Accepting a Study Place

You can accept your study place via a separate email link sent to you or in the My Studyinfo (Oma Opintopolku) service, which you can access from the front page of studyinfo.fi. To log in to the My Studyinfo service using strong authentication, you will need online banking credentials, a mobile certificate, or a chip-enabled ID card. Please also follow the instructions provided by the educational institution regarding accepting the study place.

Please accept the study place no later than 25 June 2026. If you do not accept the place by the deadline, you will lose the offered study place.

Huom! Note: This message has been sent from the studyinfo.fiservice. Please do not reply to this automated message.

Congratulations!

You have been accepted to study in the above-mentioned programme in the spring 2026 joint application to upper secondary education and preparatory education.

Please remember to accept your study place no later than 25th of June 2026. If you do not accept the place by the deadline, you will lose the study place reserved for you.

You will receive an email containing a link through which you can accept the study place. Alternatively, you may accept the study place by logging in to the My Studyinfo (Oma Opintopolku) service. Logging in requires strong authentication (e.g. online banking credentials). Please note that the educational institution may also require you to accept the study place in writing or by registering in person. Carefully read the instructions in the admission letter sent to you by the institution by post or email.

This letter also contains information about the status of your other application preferences in the student admission process. Application preferences ranked lower than the one to which you have been accepted have been automatically cancelled, and you can no longer be accepted for them. However, you may still be accepted from a waiting list to any application preferences ranked higher. Information about the admission criteria and scoring factors can be found in Studyinfo under Student admission in the joint application to upper secondary education and preparatory education.

How to Read the Table

Your Application Preferences

All your application preferences in the order you listed them on your application.

Accepted / All Applicants

The number of applicants accepted to the programme in relation to the total number of applicants.

Your Score / Lowest Accepted Score

The total score consists of several components. Information about the criteria and scoring factors can be found in Studyinfo under Student admission in the joint application to upper secondary education and preparatory education. The lowest accepted score is the minimum score with which applicants have been admitted to the programme. If you applied with a foreign certificate, without a school-leaving certificate, or if you have studied mathematics and mother tongue according to an individualised curriculum, no score will be calculated for you.

If you applied for vocational education provided with demanding special support, preparatory education and training for work and independent living (TELMA), preparatory education for vocational education (TUVA), a folk high school study year programme or general upper secondary education in English, no score will be calculated for you.

Entrance and Aptitude Test Result

If you participated in an entrance or aptitude test, your test score is shown here. If you applied with a foreign certificate, without a school-leaving certificate, or with an individualised curriculum in mathematics and your mother tongue, you applied through discretionary admission, and no score will be calculated for you. Further information about the results can be obtained directly from the institution where you took the test.

Admission Result

The admission result may be accepted, accepted through discretionary admission, on a waiting list, you were not offered admission or cancelled. The result is marked as cancelled if you have been accepted to a higher-ranked application preference.

If your status is on a waiting list, your waiting list position is shown in this column. If your result is you were not offered admission or cancelled, you cannot be on a waiting list.

Additional Information on Admission Results

Tie-Breaking in Vocational Education

In case of a tie, applicants to vocational education are ranked according to the following criteria:

  1. Order of application preferences
  2. Score from any entrance or aptitude test
  3. Overall academic performance
  4. Weighted grades.

After this, applicants with identical scores are admitted in a random order.

Tie-Breaking in General Upper Secondary Education

Upper secondary schools admit students based on grade point average. If several applicants have the same average, the admission is decided by:

  1. Order of application preferences
  2. Grade point average calculated from academic subjects as well as physical education, crafts, home economics, music, and visual arts

After this, applicants with identical scores are admitted in a random order.

Discretionary Admission

The education provider may admit an applicant regardless of score for a special reason. If you applied through discretionary admission due to social reasons, learning difficulties, or insufficient language skills, you also participate in the normal score-based admission process. If your prior education was completed abroad, you do not have a school-leaving certificate, or you studied mathematics and your mother tongue according to an individualised curriculum, you can only be accepted through discretionary admission. In this case, the admission result is either accepted through discretionary admission or you were not offered admission.

Contact Information

Further information is available in Studyinfo (studyinfo.fi). If you need help interpreting the result letter or if there are uncertainties related to the admission that cannot be resolved by contacting the institution directly, You may contact Studyinfo application advisory services at (neuvonta@opintopolku.fi or by phone at 029 533 1010). Telephone advisory services are available on 11–12th of June from 9:30 a.m. to 1:00 p.m.

Right of Appeal

If you are dissatisfied with the result of the student admission, you may request rectification of the admission decision from the The Finnish Supervisory Agency. To do so, you must request a written decision from the educational institution whose decision you wish to appeal. The decision must be requested within 30 days of receiving information about the admission result. The request for rectification must be submitted within 14 days of receiving the decision. An appeal for rectification concerning a student admission decision must be submitted to the The Finnish Supervisory Agency. The request for rectification is made using an electronic form, but it can also be sent by email to kirjaamo@lvv.fi or by post to The Finnish Supervisory Agency, P.O. Box 20, FI-13035 LVV, Finland. More detailed instructions can be found on the website of The Finnish Supervisory Agency. If, for any reason, you do not receive the decision from the institution, please contact Studyinfo application advisory services.
